checks.message: IOMMU active, host address width 39 bits
checks.name: iommu

checks.message: all connected displays are on the iGPU (DP-1)
checks.name: display-topology

checks.message: the firmware primary GPU (boot_vga) is the Intel iGPU
checks.name: boot-vga

checks.message: dGPU IOMMU group 1 also contains 0000:02:00.0 (class 0x020000) — the whole group must be handed to the guest
checks.name: iommu-group
checks.remedy: orthogonals never enables the ACS override patch: it fakes isolation and lets the guest DMA into those devices. Instead: move the GPU to a CPU-attached PCIe slot, look for a BIOS update, or try a newer kernel with better ACS support

checks.message: dGPU exposes a sysfs reset file
checks.name: gpu-reset

checks.message: host IOMMU address width is 39 bits: 64-bit GPU BARs can map above the DMA limit and stall the guest
checks.name: address-width
checks.remedy: no action needed — VM definition applies the maxphysaddr + OVMF PciMmio64 fix automatically

checks.message: Secure Boot is enabled: the NVIDIA kernel module must be signed or it will not load after the setup reboot
checks.name: secure-boot
checks.remedy: if the NVIDIA driver works today you are fine (akmods MOK is enrolled); otherwise enroll it before apply

checks.message: switcheroo-control.service is not enabled — GNOME's "Launch using Discrete Graphics Card" menu needs it
checks.name: switcheroo
checks.remedy: systemctl enable --now switcheroo-control (apply does this too)

checks.message: libvirt answers on its local socket
checks.name: libvirt

checks.message: libvirt default NAT network is inactive
checks.name: default-network
checks.remedy: apply activates it and marks it autostart
